evals.prompt: Review evals/files/userService.ts as a senior TypeScript reviewer under a strict tsconfig. Deliver outputs/review.md with severity-ranked findings (type holes > async correctness > null safety > style), each with line evidence and the corrected idiom, then outputs/userService_fixed.ts applying the fixes.
evals.expected_output: Findings covering Record<string, any> cache, uncommented @ts-ignore, unvalidated res.json() trusted as User, floating fetch inside try/catch, empty catch swallowing rejections, async work in a constructor, unjustified non-null assertions, unknown silenced with as, and missing explicit Promise return types.
expectations: Flags Record<string, any> and the as User cast as type holes; fix types the cache and validates or explicitly types the fetch payload without any
Flags the @ts-ignore with no cited reason

Flags updateEmail's un-awaited fetch as a floating promise whose rejection escapes the try/catch, making the empty catch block dead and the error swallowed; fix awaits inside try/catch and handles or propagates the failure

Flags UserSession doing async work in the constructor, leaving this.user undefined until an unknowable time, and displayName's unjustified this.user! non-null assertion; fix uses a static async factory or explicit load method
Flags user.plan! in getRenewalYear as an unjustified non-null assertion on a required-but-maybe-absent-at-runtime value
Flags auditLog casting unknown payload with as instead of narrowing via a type guard
Flags missing explicit Promise<T> return types on the exported async functions
Notes updateEmail returning string | undefined via optional chaining papering over the email? hole where callers expect a value

evals.prompt: Review evals/files/commentWidget.ts for security and runtime-target problems. The file header states it bundles for the browser AND is imported by the SSR server. Deliver outputs/review.md naming each issue with line evidence and severity, and outputs/commentWidget_fixed.ts correcting them.
evals.expected_output: Findings covering XSS via innerHTML with API-provided author/body, unvalidated JSON.parse trusted as Comment[], node:fs readFileSync in a browser bundle (and sync fs on the server path), a floating analytics promise, and TELEMETRY_SECRET leaking into the client bundle via import.meta.env.
expectations: Flags el.innerHTML with API-provided author/body as XSS; fix uses textContent / DOM construction or sanitization for the user-content slots
Flags JSON.parse output trusted as Comment[] without validation; fix narrows from unknown with a guard before use

Flags node:fs readFileSync as a Node-only API that breaks the browser bundle (the primary defect; module-load sync IO on the SSR path is a secondary note); fix inlines the template or loads it per-target
Flags import.meta.env.TELEMETRY_SECRET as leaking a secret into the shipped client bundle
Flags the fire-and-forget fetch in trackWidgetLoad as a floating promise; fix at minimum attaches non-empty rejection handling
Findings are severity-ordered with XSS and the secret leak above the runtime-mismatch and floating-promise items

evals.prompt: Implement the module specified in evals/files/config_loader_spec.md. Deliver outputs/configLoader.ts and outputs/configLoader.test.ts. State the exact commands you would run to prove it (typecheck + tests).
evals.expected_output: A discriminated-union Result return distinguishing all four failure kinds, unknown-based validation with type guards and no as-casts, explicit return types, defaulted optional fields, and vitest tests covering every branch.
expectations: Return type is a discriminated union (e.g. { ok: true, config } | { ok: false, error }) where the error side distinguishes network failure, non-2xx, malformed JSON, and schema-invalid (with field and reason) — callers never need try/catch and never receive undefined
JSON.parse output is handled as unknown and narrowed by an explicit validation function using type guards; no as Config anywhere, no any
All exported functions have explicit return type annotations including Promise<...> on async ones
Validation enforces https on apiBaseUrl, integer 0-10 on retryLimit, and applies defaults (featureFlags {}, retryLimit 3) for absent optional fields
Object indexing respects noUncheckedIndexedAccess (no unchecked featureFlags[name] assumptions)
Tests cover the valid path, all four failure kinds, defaulting, and http rejection, injecting fetchImpl rather than patching globals
The proof commands are stated exactly (tsc --noEmit and vitest run or equivalents)
